jokes.text: 25 super-hot moms, 50 eighth grade boys, no rules.
jokes.setup: Jack describing his summer replacement shows
jokes.punchline: Describing what sounds like a deeply inappropriate reality show premise
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist premise delivered matter-of-factly by Jack. Economy is excellent - three details paint complete horrifying picture. Character-specific corporate speak about inappropriate content.

jokes.text: That doesn't mean she's not a wonderful, caring milf.
jokes.setup: Learning one of the MILF Island contestants was a prostitute
jokes.punchline: Jack defending her character while using the acronym MILF in corporate speak
jokes.explanation: Jack treating MILF Island like legitimate programming while defending a prostitute's maternal qualities. Perfect corporate doublespeak meets inappropriate content.

jokes.text: So my old tech guys were able to digitally capture seinfeld, and now we can basically make him do or say whatever we want.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining Seinfeldvision technology
jokes.punchline: Casually describing digital puppeteering of a real person as if it's normal
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on digital manipulation delivered as corporate innovation. The casual 'whatever we want' is chillingly funny.

jokes.text: Seinfeldvision montage with Jerry saying 'Yah! That's what I said' and 'I'll take that deal!'
jokes.setup: Seeing the actual Seinfeldvision promos
jokes.punchline: Jerry digitally inserted saying generic catchphrases in wrong contexts
jokes.explanation: Visual payoff to the Seinfeldvision concept. Funny seeing Jerry's mannerisms forced into wrong contexts.

jokes.text: Jerry's in europe with his family right now. But by the time he gets back, seinfeldvision will be a monster hit, and his kids'll go to school, and their friends'll say i really loved your dad in that episode of medium last night, and he'S... he's gonna love it.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining why Jerry will be okay with this
jokes.punchline: Elaborate fantasy scenario where children peer pressure Jerry into acceptance
jokes.explanation: Jack's delusional optimism escalating to imagining children as his sales force. Good structure building to the confident conclusion.

jokes.text: I started a quilt. I did yoga twice a week. I wore flip-flops in public.
jokes.setup: Liz describing her great summer
jokes.punchline: Increasingly mundane 'achievements' presented as personal growth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon character comedy. The escalation to flip-flops as a major life change is perfectly pathetic and relatable.

jokes.text: If there's two things i'm certain of, one is you will never finish that quilt
jokes.setup: Jack about to give Liz life advice
jokes.punchline: Immediately attacking her quilt project with surgical precision
jokes.explanation: Jack's brutal honesty presented as friendly advice. Perfect callback timing and character-specific cruelty disguised as wisdom.

jokes.text: Lemon, women your age are more likely to be mauled at the zoo than get married.
jokes.setup: Liz saying she's positive about meeting someone
jokes.punchline: Jack delivering a brutal fake statistic with corporate confidence
jokes.explanation: One of the episode's best lines. Fake statistic format, absurd comparison, delivered with Jack's characteristic confidence. Highly quotable and perfectly mean.

jokes.text: Put floyd's name on that quilt.
jokes.setup: Jack walking away from devastating Liz
jokes.punchline: Casual suggestion to memorialize her doomed relationship on her doomed quilt
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that ties together two separate insults. Economy is excellent - six words that devastate on multiple levels.

jokes.text: Yo, ken, I'm gon use this whole kitchen area as my bathroom. Spread the word.
jokes.setup: Tracy arriving at work displaced from home
jokes.punchline: Immediately claiming the kitchen as his bathroom and treating it like official business
jokes.explanation: Pure Tracy Jordan logic - treating workplace as home with zero consideration for others. 'Spread the word' makes it official business.

jokes.text: She froze my credit and she got custody of grizz.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining his marital troubles
jokes.punchline: Treating his bodyguard like a pet in custody arrangements
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist take on divorce proceedings. The casual treatment of Grizz as property is perfectly wrong and funny.

jokes.text: So you know I like to minister to transvestite prostitutes. I don't think I did know that, no.
jokes.setup: Tracy assuming Liz knows his charitable work
jokes.punchline: Liz's perfectly reasonable response to this insane assumption
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline with Tracy's casual assumption and Liz's deadpan reality check. Great contrast between characters.

jokes.text: He doesn't mess with 'em. He just tries to get 'em into computer school.
jokes.setup: Kenneth defending Tracy's ministry work
jokes.punchline: The specific career goal of computer school for transvestite prostitutes
jokes.explanation: Kenneth's innocent delivery of Tracy's bizarre charity work. Computer school is such a specific, mundane goal for such an absurd situation.

jokes.text: You can be a freaky deeky and do data entry. What about court reporting? Believe in yourself.
jokes.setup: Tracy recounting his motivational speech
jokes.punchline: Combining transgender acceptance with mundane career counseling
jokes.explanation: Tracy's progressive acceptance mixed with hilariously specific career advice. 'Freaky deeky' is a perfect Tracy coinage.

jokes.text: they confused 'shim' with mrs. Jordan, yes, ma'am. And they think she looks better here than she did at the 2004 grammys.
jokes.setup: Explaining the paparazzi photo mixup
jokes.punchline: The tabloid thinks the prostitute looks better than his actual wife at a major event
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the absurd situation. The specific Grammy reference adds believable detail to the ridiculous premise.

jokes.text: And they think she looks better here than she did at the 2004 grammys.
jokes.setup: The mistaken identity scandal
jokes.punchline: Adding insult to injury about his wife's appearance
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that deepens the insult while staying true to Tracy's obliviousness.

jokes.text: So he's, like, my office wife? Sure. Let's go with that.
jokes.setup: Kenneth trying to understand his new role with Tracy
jokes.punchline: Liz's exhausted acceptance of this weird workplace arrangement
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's management style - just go with whatever weird thing is happening. Kenneth's innocent question is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Kenneth parcell, will you take this ring... and sell it in the jewish part of midtown, and use the money to get us a nintendo wii? Yes. Yes. A thousand times yes!
jokes.setup: Tracy proposing marriage parody to Kenneth
jokes.punchline: The 'romantic' request is to pawn jewelry for a gaming system
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of marriage proposals with Tracy's priorities and Kenneth's enthusiasm. The escalation from romantic to materialistic is well-executed.

jokes.text: when life keeps handing you anchovies just cover them up with some extra cheese and make a pizza
jokes.setup: Jenna sharing wisdom from her Broadway experience
jokes.punchline: Completely garbled life advice that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Pure Jenna - trying to sound profound but delivering nonsense. The pizza metaphor attempting to be inspirational is perfectly wrong.

jokes.text: 'cause my play was amazing. There is nothing like the thrill of doing a live show on broadway.
jokes.setup: Jenna interrupting Liz about Floyd
jokes.punchline: Immediately making it about herself and her theater experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna character moment of narcissistic interruption, but not particularly original or memorable.

jokes.text: Liz, I had to eat four slices of pizza on stage, each performance... jenna, that's 32 pieces of pizza a week! No, that can't be right.
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining her weight gain from Broadway show
jokes.punchline: Jenna refusing to accept basic math about her pizza consumption
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Jenna's denial. Liz doing the math and Jenna rejecting reality is perfect for both characters.

jokes.text: it's like I flipped the eating switch, and I can't flip it back.
jokes.setup: Jenna trying to explain her eating problem
jokes.punchline: Treating eating like a simple on/off mechanism
jokes.explanation: Jenna's oversimplified understanding of nutrition and self-control. The switch metaphor is appropriately shallow for the character.

jokes.text: oh it's worse from behind
jokes.setup: Jenna asking for help with weight
jokes.punchline: Immediate regret upon seeing herself from different angle
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag with good economy but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: She need to lose thirty pounds or gain sixty. anything between it, not place in television
jokes.setup: Jack assessing Jenna's weight situation
jokes.punchline: Brutal TV industry logic delivered as business advice
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly harsh TV industry reality delivered with Jack's characteristic corporate efficiency. The specific pound amounts make it hilariously clinical.

jokes.text: Yowza!! Oh! hey Mrs Michelin, I love your ty
jokes.setup: Pete encountering Jenna
jokes.punchline: Physical reaction to her weight gain plus calling her Mrs. Michelin (tire company mascot)
jokes.explanation: Basic fat joke with Michelin Man reference. Functional but not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: Floyd the black guy. okay, you don't care. i don't care either... so great
jokes.setup: Liz trying to seem fine about her breakup to the writers
jokes.punchline: Awkward overselling of how fine she is when no one cares
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Liz's emotional state. The awkward overselling when no one asked is realistic and uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Angie kept my sharp image white noise aroma therapy machine
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining why he can't sleep
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously specific bougie sleep aid dependency
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy character detail - the overly specific brand name and combination of wellness trends is perfectly nouveau riche.

jokes.text: I went with you to your black vampire movie. But I guess I'll just tell my friends that you have a migraine.
jokes.setup: Kenneth trying to guilt Tracy into social obligations
jokes.punchline: Kenneth passive-aggressively threatening to make excuses for Tracy
jokes.explanation: Kenneth showing unexpected passive-aggressive behavior while maintaining his sweet demeanor. 'Black vampire movie' is a good specific detail.

jokes.text: I got a bone to pick with that guy. I don't know which bone i'm gonna pick, but he's got some bones, and I'm gonna pick one.
jokes.setup: Jerry angry about Seinfeldvision
jokes.punchline: Over-explaining the bone-picking metaphor to absurd lengths
jokes.explanation: Good Seinfeld-style over-analysis of a common phrase. The escalation and repetition are character-appropriate and funny.

jokes.text: This veil costs more than my couch. Is that comedy, or do you really have a $300 couch? Both.
jokes.setup: Liz commenting on veil prices
jokes.punchline: Cerie questioning if it's a joke, Liz confirming her sad reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect economic disparity joke between characters. Liz's 'Both' is economical and perfectly captures her situation.

jokes.text: This is my husband, saul rosenbear, and this is his son richard from a previous marriage. And then he cheated on me with a lamb.
jokes.setup: Store clerk sharing her wedding story
jokes.punchline: Normal family introduction escalating to bestiality betrayal
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from mundane to absurd. The matter-of-fact delivery of the lamb betrayal is perfectly unexpected and dark.

jokes.text: This is my husnd, saul rosenbear, and this is his son richard from a previous marriage. And then he cheated on me with a lamb.
jokes.setup: Liz pretending to be a bride with a backstory
jokes.punchline: The escalation to bestiality
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that shows Liz's dark imagination when role-playing

jokes.text: He looks the way you did when I tried to hold your hand on the jet.
jokes.setup: Describing Jerry's emotional state
jokes.punchline: Reference to Jack's discomfort with physical affection
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Jack's emotional distance. Jonathan's observational comparison is well-crafted.

jokes.text: If I cover my good eye, you look just like courtney cox.
jokes.setup: Clerk commenting on Liz in wedding dress
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment requiring visual impairment
jokes.explanation: Brutal backhanded compliment. The specific requirement of covering her good eye makes it perfectly insulting yet somehow meant as nice.

jokes.text: Oh, no. Aris would kill me if I ever bought a dress on sale. I can'T.
jokes.setup: Cerie considering a discounted dress
jokes.punchline: Being forbidden from saving money by wealthy fiancé
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Cerie's wealth bubble. The idea that buying on sale would be offensive to her fiancé is perfectly privileged.

jokes.text: Still talking? oh, no, no, no, no. I haven't talked to him since--whew! August...9th? 4:17 P.M.? It's not over.
jokes.setup: Liz claiming to be over Floyd
jokes.punchline: Knowing the exact minute of their last conversation proves otherwise
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Liz's denial. Jerry's immediate 'It's not over' is the perfect punchline to her obvious lie.

jokes.text: No, it's not over till you pick up the phone, you say, 'I don't love you anymore, ' they say, 'I don't love you anymore either.' You go, 'great, I'll pick you up in 20. Let's grab a scone.'
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining how breakups really work
jokes.punchline: The immediate reconciliation over baked goods
jokes.explanation: Good Seinfeld-style relationship observation. The escalation to immediate reconciliation over scones is absurd and accurate.

jokes.text: I need you to go to my house and pretend you're doing a survey for the ranford group. And then ask my wife if she's sleeping with D.L. Hughley!
jokes.setup: Tracy asking Liz for a favor
jokes.punchline: Elaborate spy mission to investigate marital infidelity with specific comedian
jokes.explanation: Great Tracy paranoia with specific random celebrity and elaborate scheme. The fake survey company name adds believable detail.

jokes.text: Remember that night we had a three-way with elayne boosler? I don't think that was me. Oh, yeah. You know what? I think that was a mirror.
jokes.setup: Tracy reminiscing about shared experience with Jerry
jokes.punchline: Jerry's denial leading to Tracy's realization it was masturbation
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup and punchline. The escalation from threesome to realizing it was just him and a mirror is perfectly absurd and quotable.

jokes.text: I am conducting a survey for the ranford group. And, uh...how old are you? And your weight? And when was the last time you had intercourse? Who is this? Who are you? I'm your worst nightmare, is who I is!
jokes.setup: Liz's fake survey going wrong
jokes.punchline: Tracy's ridiculous threatening persona
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the survey scheme gone wrong. Tracy's 'who I is' grammar and tough guy act is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: Svenborgia? No. Better. But I can't tell you.
jokes.setup: Jack guessing Jerry's vacation destination
jokes.punchline: Jerry being secretive about rich people country
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist concept of countries only rich people know about. Jerry's secretive superiority is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Jerry, don't be difficult. The fact of the matter is that seinfeldvision is perfectly legal, and there's nothing you can do to stop us.
jokes.setup: Jack trying to strong-arm Jerry
jokes.punchline: Treating digital puppeteering as normal business practice
jokes.explanation: Good Jack character moment showing his corporate bullying style. The casual dismissal of Jerry's rights is perfectly corporate.

jokes.text: How 'bout I buy nbc and turn it into the biggest lane bryant in midtown?
jokes.setup: Jerry threatening to buy NBC
jokes.punchline: Turning it into a plus-size women's clothing store
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with specific, unexpected threat. Lane Bryant is a perfect choice for maximum insult to network prestige.

jokes.text: You're gonna buy nbc, like you've got $4 million just laying around...
jokes.setup: Jack dismissing Jerry's threat
jokes.punchline: Severely underestimating the cost of a major network
jokes.explanation: Great joke showing Jack's business incompetence. $4 million for NBC is hilariously off by several billion dollars.

jokes.text: Remember, st. Barts, I saved your life from that shark? All right, jack. I'll come back. But I still think you shot a dolphin.
jokes.setup: Jack referencing shared adventure
jokes.punchline: Jerry's different memory of the shark incident
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist backstory between characters. The disagreement over shark vs dolphin is perfectly ambiguous and funny.

jokes.text: a lot of women buy their perfect dress when they see it, and then just trust the fact that the husband will come.
jokes.setup: Saleswoman encouraging dress purchase
jokes.punchline: Build-it-and-he-will-come approach to marriage
jokes.explanation: Decent saleswoman logic that's both practical and sad. The reverse order of dress-then-husband is realistically desperate.

jokes.text: I'm gonna get the wedding dress, and then I'm gonna have a baby, and then I'm gonna die, and then I'm gonna meet a super-cute guy in heaven.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining her life plan
jokes.punchline: Including death in timeline and meeting someone in afterlife
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Liz's desperation. The matter-of-fact inclusion of death and heavenly dating is perfectly absurd yet emotionally honest.

jokes.text: Number one--kill seinfeld. Number two--kill seinfeld, then kill myself. Number three--kill seinfeld, flee to svenborgia, then kill myself.
jokes.setup: Jack brainstorming solutions
jokes.punchline: All solutions involve murder, with escalating complexity
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Jack's crisis mode. The progression from simple murder to international murder-suicide is perfectly unhinged.

jokes.text: you seduce seinfeld. Now, why is me seducing seinfeld all the way at 70?
jokes.setup: Jack continuing his brainstorm
jokes.punchline: Questioning the ranking of his own sexual appeal as business solution
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jack vanity moment. His offense at the low ranking of his seduction plan shows perfect character priorities.

jokes.text: I picked out all these throw pillows for in here, and you didn't even notice!
jokes.setup: Kenneth upset with Tracy
jokes.punchline: Domestic complaint about home decoration like a spurned spouse
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the office wife dynamic. Kenneth's genuine hurt over decorating efforts is perfectly domestic and weird.

jokes.text: How did I lose 25 pounds in one day? I didn'T. It's visual trickery. Drawing the eye up.
jokes.setup: Jenna addressing her apparent weight loss
jokes.punchline: Admitting to optical illusion rather than actual fitness
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna character moment showing her vanity and theatrical solutions to practical problems. Visual trickery over actual effort is very her.

jokes.text: I just bought a dress. Because, you know, I don't need society's permission to buy a white dress. In korea, they wear white to funerals.
jokes.setup: Liz defending her wedding dress purchase
jokes.punchline: Rationalizing with random cultural fact about funeral attire
jokes.explanation: Good Liz rationalization escalating to absurd cultural justification. The Korea funeral fact is the kind of random knowledge she would use defensively.

jokes.text: That, if need be, I will marry myself.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining her statement
jokes.punchline: Threatening self-marriage as ultimate independence
jokes.explanation: Decent Liz moment of defensive independence. Self-marriage threat is appropriately pathetic for her emotional state.

jokes.text: Yeah, chocolate rain, maybe I am.
jokes.setup: Frank questioning her wearing the dress all day
jokes.punchline: Liz using random internet meme reference as dismissive comeback
jokes.explanation: Weak joke relying on random pop culture reference. Doesn't serve character or story, just name-dropping viral video for no clear reason.

jokes.text: What is the deal with my life? Are you imitating me? No! This is what i sound like when I cry!
jokes.setup: Liz having breakdown in Seinfeld cadence
jokes.punchline: Jerry calling out the impression, Liz denying while continuing it
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-joke about Seinfeld's speaking pattern. Liz unconsciously imitating while crying is brilliant, and Jerry's reaction is perfect.

jokes.text: I think I'm a little insulted! You're insulted?!
jokes.setup: Jerry expressing hurt at being imitated
jokes.punchline: Liz incredulous at his priorities while doing more Seinfeld voice
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the meta-joke. Jerry being insulted while Liz continues the impression unconsciously extends the comedy effectively.

jokes.text: I got nothin'.
jokes.setup: Jack facing Jerry with no plan
jokes.punchline: Complete admission of defeat in three words
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy showing Jack's rare moment of complete defeat. The simplicity after all his elaborate schemes is effective.

jokes.text: God--oh, god, jerry! I got nothing! You've got to do this for me, please! Oh, god, I've already sold the ad time!
jokes.setup: Jack's complete breakdown
jokes.punchline: Desperate pleading after his confident corporate persona crumbles
jokes.explanation: Good character breakdown moment. Jack's desperation after his usual confidence fails shows good character development.

jokes.text: Roker in a bee costume. Grenyarnia.
jokes.setup: Final negotiation terms
jokes.punchline: Al Roker costume demand and the secret rich country reveal
jokes.explanation: Great callback resolution with perfect economy. Roker in bee costume is absurd and specific, Grenyarnia payoff is satisfying.

jokes.text: Oh, no! Did a korean person die?
jokes.setup: Tracy seeing Liz in white dress
jokes.punchline: Remembering her Korean funeral fact literally
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with Tracy taking Liz's rationalization literally. Perfect economy and character-appropriate misunderstanding.

jokes.text: I know what me and kenneth have looks perfect on the outside, but it's work, damn it. It's work.
jokes.setup: Tracy giving Liz relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Treating his office wife arrangement as a real marriage with real struggles
jokes.explanation: Good callback extending the office marriage metaphor. Tracy's sincere relationship advice about his fake marriage is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Lemon, don't ever say you're just you. Because you are better than you.
jokes.setup: Jack giving Liz a pep talk
jokes.punchline: Confusing motivational logic that sounds profound but makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Good Jack pseudo-wisdom that sounds motivational but is grammatically nonsensical. Perfect character moment of corporate speak meeting friendship.

jokes.text: $4, 000 ham napkin.
jokes.setup: Visual of expensive dress being used as napkin
jokes.punchline: Jack's casual assessment of the dress as overpriced food accessory
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy describing the visual gag. Jack reducing Liz's emotional purchase to its practical ham-wiping function is brutally funny.

jokes.text: I look pretty, though, right? Don't push it, lemon.
jokes.setup: Liz seeking validation in her messy state
jokes.punchline: Jack's immediate shutdown of her fishing for compliments
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack/Liz dynamic. Jack's refusal to indulge her post-breakdown validation seeking is perfectly in character and economical.
